titleStmt.title: Letter from William Sheehan to Lord Wimborne, 26 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Sheehan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Sheehan (b.1877), clerk of the Fermoy Urban District, to Ivor Guest, the Lord Wimborne, (1873-1939), lord lieutenant of Ireland. The letter concerns a malicious injury claim made by Sarah J. Rowe (b.1868) due to the death of her husband William N. Rowe (1867-1916), RIC constable who was shot in an attempt to arrest members of the Kent family in connection with Sinn Fein activity in County Cork. Thomas Kent (1865-1916) was later executed for the murder of Constable Rowe. The letter also has a copy of a resolution made regarding the case, calling on the authorities to pay Mrs Rowe some form of compensation. It goes on to state that the application for the fund to be levied off the rate payers should be withdrawn, so not to disturb the peace.Ivor Churchill Guest, 1st Viscount Wimborne was appointed the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland in 1915. Guest worked closely alongside Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) in the administration of Ireland. Following the suppression of the Rising, Guest, under pressure, was forced to resign as Lord Lieutenant. A later inquiry into the Rising exonerated Guest and held Augustine Birrell (1850-1933) chiefly responsible for the events that occurred. Guest would continue to serve as Lord Lieutenant until 1918.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5612/9090

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Council Chamber, Fermoy, 26th May 1916
To. His Excellency Baron Wimborne Lord Lieutenant of Ireland. Dublin Castle. May it please Your Excellency: I am directed by the Fermoy Urban
Council to transmit to you the undermentioned Resolution in
reference to the Malicious Injury Claim lodged by Mrs Rowe for
Â£2500 compensation for the death of her husband the late Head Const
Rowe, which occurred on the 2nd inst. I remain,
Your Excellency's,
Most Obedient Servant Wm Sheehan
Town Clerk. I ack
II
Please say what pension
may be paid to Mrs Rowe under
the R.I.C. Acts. 29-5-16
Copy Resolution 'That solely with a view to the continuance of the
'good, peaceable, and law abiding character of this District, and to
'obviate any cause of friction or ill feeling, We most earnestly urge
'on the Authorities to provide out of some fund at their disposal such
'compensation to the Family of the late Head Constable Rowe R.I.C,
'Fermoy, as they may justly be considered entutled to for his loss which
'we all regret, and that the pending application of his Widow for
'compensation to be levied off the Ratepayers should be withdrawn.
'We do not desire to express any opinion as to the merits or otherwise
'of the application, but we consider that the levying of such a Decree
'for compensation off the Ratepayers would inevitably provoke a
'disturbance of the peace of the District and lead to a repetition of
'the unfortunate occurrences with which this Barony of Condons and
Clongibbons was visited in the Eighties'.
